header.subtitle: 2-channel relay module — Modbus RTU control
lang.label: Language:
conn.banner_sub_disconnected: Click Connect to start communication with the module.
conn.bar_disconnected: ○ DISCONNECTED — select COM port and click Connect
hint.factory_addr: Tip: factory address is FF (255). After setting a new address, connect using that address (e.g. 01).
addr.help: Write to register 0 with slave=0 (broadcast). After change, disconnect and connect with the new address.
info.format: Format: 9600 baud, 8N1 (8 data bits, no parity, 1 stop bit)
info.factory: Factory address of new module: 0xFF (255). After setup may be e.g. 0x01
info.sensor: Optional T/H sensor: Slave ID 0x02 (if installed)
control.lock_banner: ⚠  CONTROL LOCKED — connect to the module first (Connection tab)
advanced.help: Select a Modbus function, address, count, and values (comma-separated). Read coils/inputs: count=8. Write multiple coils (0x0F): values 1,1,1,1,1,1,1,1 or single 1/0.
about.body:
303Modbus13 RTU Control v1.0

Application for control and diagnostics of the
Ideaflex 303Modbus13 relay module over Modbus RTU.

Features:
  • Control 2 relays (coils 0–1)
  • Read 2 opto inputs (discrete inputs 0–1)
  • Optional temperature and humidity sensor
  • Change Modbus slave address
  • Advanced console — Modbus FC 01–06, 0F, 10

User guide: docs/USER_GUIDE.md
Register map: docs/REGISTERS.md

Open source — MIT License (see LICENSE file).
about.step1: 1. Connect the module to a COM port (e.g. via USB-RS485).
about.step2: 2. Select COM port and click Connect.
about.step3: 3. On the Control tab, turn relays on/off.
about.step4: 4. Input status updates after each relay command; use Refresh for a full readout.
log.scan_start: Scanning: looking for module on COM ports...
log.scan_not_found: Scan finished — module did not respond.
log.addr_changed: Address changed to 0x{addr:02X}. Reconnect with the new address.
log.inputs_ok_none: Input read OK (FC 0x02): {inputs} — no signal on inputs (check IN/COM wiring)
log.inputs_unavailable: Input read unavailable — use Read inputs.
log.state_unavailable: Status read unavailable — showing last known state (write works).
detail.scanning: scanning COM ports and slave addresses...
msg.error_slave: Slave ID must be hex 00–FF (e.g. FF or 01).
msg.error_addr_int: Address and count must be positive integers.
msg.error_write_values: For write operations, enter values (e.g. 1 for ON, 0 for OFF).
msg.error_slave_optional: Slave ID must be hex 00–FF or empty.
msg.error_addr_hex: Address must be a hex number.
msg.conn_error_hint:
{err}

Try:
• Slave ID = FF (factory address 255)
• Search for module button
• Different COM port in Device Manager
• Close other programs using the serial port
msg.busy: Wait for the current operation to finish or click Cancel.
msg.not_found:
Module did not respond on any COM port.

Check:
• USB-RS485 cable and module power
• COM port in Device Manager
• RS-485 A/B wiring
